Subsection.Text: If a supply of a residential complex is made to two or more individuals, the references in subsection (5) to a particular individual shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but only the particular individual that applied for the rebate under section 254 may apply for the rebate under subsection (5).

A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after the day on which ownership of the complex is transferred to the person.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate where section 254.1 applies — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into on or before May 2, 2006, between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex that is a single unit residential complex or a residential condo-minium unit, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which the residential unit forming part of the complex is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of the complex is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(1) to have made and received a supply of the complex as a consequence of giving possession of the complex to the particular person under the agreement and to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 7%, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (4),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: pay a rebate to the particular person, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.4(1), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the rebate under subsection 254.1(2) that the particular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under subsection 256.2(4) or 256.4(1), in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (c), pay a rebate to the builder, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.4(1), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the complex at the time that the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (c),
is the amount determined for A under paragraph (e), and
is the amount of the rebate, if any, that the builder is entitled to claim under subsection 256.2(4).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate where section 254.1 does not apply — 2008 rate reduction
Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into on or before May 2, 2006, between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex that is a single unit residential complex or a residential condo-minium unit, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which the residential unit forming part of the complex is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of the complex is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(1) to have made and received a supply of the complex as a consequence of giving possession of the complex to the particular person under the agreement and to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 7%, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person is not entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (4),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: pay a rebate to the particular person, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.4(2), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attrib-utable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under subsection 256.4(2), in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (c), pay a rebate to the builder, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.4(2), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the complex at the time the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (c),

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attrib-utable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107.

Subsection.Text: If the supplies described in subsection (1) or (2) are made to two or more individuals, the references in that subsection to a particular person shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but, in the case of a rebate where paragraph (1)(e) applies, only the individual that applied for the rebate under section 254.1 may apply for the rebate under subsection (1).
A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rebate to a person other than the builder of the complex, the day on which possession of the complex is transferred to the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rebate to the builder of the complex, the end of the month in which the tax referred to in paragraph (1)(c) or (2)(c) is deemed to have been paid by the builder.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate for purchaser — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: Where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex, other than a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, or an addition to it, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(3) or (4) to have made and received a supply of the complex or the addition as a consequence of giving possession

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: of the residential unit to the particular person under the agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition to another person under an agreement described in paragraph (a) entered into between the other person and the builder,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 7%, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have paid the tax referred to in paragraph (d) on or after January 1, 2008, it is the case that the builder and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular person entered into the agreement on or before May 2, 2006, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person, other than the particular person, on or before May 2, 2006, entered into an agreement described in paragraph (a) in respect of a residential unit situated in the residential complex or in the addition that the builder is deemed to have supplied (as described in paragraph (c)) and that agreement was not terminated before July 1, 2006,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (3),

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular person is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex, pay a rebate to the particular person, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.5(1), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex or the addition, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the rebate under subsection 254.1 that the particular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex, and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular person is not entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex, pay a rebate to the particular person, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.5(1), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of a building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex or the addition, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107.

Subsection.Text: If the supplies described in subsection (1) are made to two or more individuals, the references in that subsection to a particular person shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but, in the case of a rebate under paragraph (1)(f), only the individ-ual that applied for the rebate under section 254.1 may apply for the rebate under that paragraph.

A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after the day on which possession of the unit referred to in paragraph (1)(b) is transferred to the person.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate for builder — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex, other than a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, or an addition to it, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or a supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(3) or (4) to have made and received on or after January 1, 2008, a supply of the complex or the addition as a consequence of giving possession

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: of the residential unit to the particular person under the agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition to another person under an agreement described in paragraph (a) entered into between the other person and the builder,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular person entered into the agreement on or before May 2, 2006, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person, other than the particular person, on or before May 2, 2006, entered into an agreement described in paragraph (a) in respect of a residential unit situated in the residential complex or in the addition that the builder is deemed to have supplied (as described in paragraph (b)) and that agreement was not terminated before July 1, 2006,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply referred to in paragraph (b) calculated at the rate of 7%, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under subsection 256.2(4) or 256.6(1), in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (d),

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (2), pay a rebate to the builder, in addition to the rebate payable under subsection 256.6(1), equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the fair market value of the complex or, if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, of the addition, at the time the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (b),
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of a complex, the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by a person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the person of the building or part of it forming part of the complex or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by a person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the person of the building or part of it forming part of the addition or of any other structure that forms part of the addition, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 115, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 107, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the rebate, if any, under subsection 256.2(4) that the builder is entitled to claim in respect of the complex or, if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, the addition.

Subsection.Text: A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex or an addition to it shall not be paid to a builder, unless the builder files an application for the rebate within two years after the end of the month in which tax referred to in subsection (1) is deemed to have been paid by the builder.
If a particular person, other than a cooperative housing corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement of purchase and sale, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, is the recipient of a taxable supply by way of sale from another person of a residential complex in respect of which ownership and possession under the agreement are transferred to the particular person on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all of the tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection, in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (b),

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (7), pay a rebate to the particular person equal to 1% of the value of the consideration for the supply.

Subsection.Text: If a particular person, other than a cooperative housing corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement of purchase and sale, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, is the recipient of a taxable supply by way of sale from another person of a residential complex in respect of which ownership and possession under the agreement are transferred to the particular person on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all of the tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 256.2(3) in respect of any residential unit situated in the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (7), pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the consideration payable for the supply to the particular person of the complex, and
is the amount of the rebate under subsection 256.2(3) that the particular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex.

Subsection.Text: If a particular person, other than a cooperative housing corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement of purchase and sale, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, is the recipient of a taxable supply by way of sale from another person of a residential complex in respect of which ownership and possession under the agreement are transferred to the particular person on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all of the tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is entitled to claim a rebate under section 259 in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (b) and is not entitled to claim any input tax credit or any other rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection, in respect of that tax,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (7), pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the consideration payable for the supply to the particular person of the complex, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in the case where the complex is situated in a participating province, the amount of the rebate under section 259 that the particular person would have been entitled to claim if no tax under subsection 165(2) would have been payable or paid in respect of the complex, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount of the rebate under section 259 that the partic-ular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex.

Subsection.Text: If a cooperative housing corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement of purchase and sale, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, is the recipient of a taxable supply by way of sale from another person of a residential complex in respect of which ownership and possession under the agreement are transferred to the corporation on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all of the tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under section 256.2 or 259, in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (b),

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (7), pay a rebate to the corporation equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the consideration payable for the supply, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the corporation is entitled to claim a rebate under section 259 in respect of the complex,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in the case where the complex is situated in a participating province, the amount of the rebate under section 259 that the corporation would have been entitled to claim if no tax under subsection 165(2) would have been payable or paid in respect of the complex, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount of the rebate under section 259 that the corporation is entitled to claim in respect of the complex,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 36% of the tax paid under subsection 165(1) by the corporation in respect of the supply if the corporation is not entitled to claim a rebate under section 259 in respect of the complex, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the corporation is entitled to, or can reasonably expect to be entitled to, claim a rebate under section 256.2 in respect of any residential unit situated in the complex, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: it is the case that, or it can reasonably be expected that, a share of the capital stock of the corporation is or will be sold to an individual for the purpose of using a residential unit in the complex as the primary place of residence of the individual, or of a relation (as defined in subsection 255(1)) of the individual, and that the individual is or will be entitled to claim a rebate under section 255 in respect of the share, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, zero.

Subsection.Text: If a particular individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement of purchase and sale, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, is the recipient of a taxable supply by way of sale from another person of a residential complex in respect of which ownership and possession under the agreement are transferred to the particular individ-ual on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has paid all of the tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254(2) in respect of the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (7), pay a rebate to the particular individual equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable for the supply to the particular individual of the complex or for any other taxable supply to the particular individual of an interest in the complex in respect of which the particular individual has paid tax under subsection 165(1) calculated at the rate of 6%, and
is the amount of the rebate under subsection 254(2) that the particular individual is entitled to claim in respect of the complex.

Subsection.Text: If a supply of a residential complex is made to two or more individuals, the references in subsection (5) to a particular individual shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but only the particular individual that applied for the rebate under section 254 may apply for the rebate under subsection (5).

A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after the day on which ownership of the complex is transferred to the person.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate where section 254.1 applies — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex that is a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which the residential unit forming part of the complex is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of the complex is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(1) to have made and received a supply of the complex as a consequence of giving possession of the complex to the particular person under the agreement and to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (4),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the rebate under subsection 254.1(2) that the particular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under subsection 256.2(4), in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (c), pay a rebate to the builder equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the complex at the time that the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (c),
is the amount determined for A under paragraph (e), and
is the amount of the rebate, if any, that the builder is entitled to claim under subsection 256.2(4).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate where section 254.1 does not apply — 2008 rate reduction
Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex that is a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which the residential unit forming part of the complex is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of the complex is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(1) to have made and received a supply of the complex as a consequence of giving possession of the complex to the particular person under the agreement and to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person is not entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (4),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attribut-able to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection, in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (c), pay a rebate to the builder equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the complex at the time the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (c),

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attribut-able to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106.

Subsection.Text: If the supplies described in subsection (1) or (2) are made to two or more individuals, the references in that subsection to a particular person shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but, in the case of a rebate where paragraph (1)(e) applies, only the individual that applied for the rebate under section 254.1 may apply for the rebate under subsection (1).
A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rebate to a person other than the builder of the complex, the day on which possession of the complex is transferred to the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rebate to the builder of the complex, the end of the month in which the tax referred to in paragraph (1)(c) or (2)(c) is deemed to have been paid by the builder.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate for purchaser — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: Where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex, other than a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, or an addition to it, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or an exempt supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: possession of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is given to the particular person under the agreement on or after January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(3) or (4) to have made and received a supply of the complex or the addition as a consequence of giving possession

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: of the residential unit to the particular person under the agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition to another person under an agreement described in paragraph (a) entered into between the other person and the builder,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have paid the tax referred to in paragraph (d) on or after January 1, 2008, it is the case that the builder and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular person entered into the agreement after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person, other than the particular person, after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, entered into an agreement described in paragraph (a) in respect of a residential unit situated in the residential complex or in the addition that the builder is deemed to have supplied (as described in paragraph (c)) and that agreement was not terminated before January 1, 2008,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (3),

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular person is entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex, pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of the building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex or the addition, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the rebate under section 254.1 that the particular person is entitled to claim in respect of the complex, and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular person is not entitled to claim a rebate under subsection 254.1(2) in respect of the complex, pay a rebate to the particular person equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by the particular person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the particular person of the building or part of a building referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or of any other structure that forms part of the complex or the addition, other than consideration that can reasonably be regarded as rent for the supplies of the land attributable to the complex or as consideration for the supply of an option to purchase that land, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106.

Subsection.Text: If the supplies described in subsection (1) are made to two or more individuals, the references in that subsection to a particular person shall be read as references to all of those individuals as a group, but, in the case of a rebate under paragraph (1)(f), only the individ-ual that applied for the rebate under section 254.1 may apply for the rebate under that paragraph.

A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex shall not be paid to a person, unless the person files an application for the rebate within two years after the day on which possession of the unit referred to in paragraph (1)(b) is transferred to the person.

Section.MarginalNote: Transitional rebate for builder — 2008 rate reduction

Subsection.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement, evidenced in writing, entered into between a particular person and a builder of a residential complex, other than a single unit residential complex or a residential condominium unit, or an addition to it, the particular person is the recipient of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of lease of the land forming part of the complex or a supply of such a lease by way of assignment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an exempt supply by way of sale of the building or part of it in which a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition is situated,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed under subsection 191(3) or (4) to have made and received on or after January 1, 2008, a supply of the complex or the addition as a consequence of giving possession

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: of the residential unit to the particular person under the agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: of a residential unit forming part of the complex or the addition to another person under an agreement described in paragraph (a) entered into between the other person and the builder,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the builder and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular person entered into the agreement after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person, other than the particular person, after May 2, 2006, but on or before October 30, 2007, entered into an agreement described in paragraph (a) in respect of a residential unit situated in the residential complex or in the addition that the builder is deemed to have supplied (as described in paragraph (b)) and that agreement was not terminated before January 1, 2008,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is deemed to have paid tax under subsection 165(1) in respect of the supply referred to in paragraph (b) calculated at the rate of 6%, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the builder is not entitled to claim an input tax credit or a rebate, other than a rebate under this subsection or under subsection 256.2(4), in respect of the tax referred to in paragraph (d),

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister shall, subject to subsection (2), pay a rebate to the builder equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is the fair market value of the complex or, if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, of the addition, at the time the builder is deemed to have made the supply referred to in paragraph (b),
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of a complex, the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by a person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the person of the building or part of it forming part of the complex or of any other structure that forms part of the complex, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, the total of all amounts, each of which is the consideration payable by a person to the builder for the supply by way of sale to the person of the building or part of it forming part of the addition or of any other structure that forms part of the addition, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the complex is situated in a participating province, 114, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 106, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the rebate, if any, under subsection 256.2(4) that the builder is entitled to claim in respect of the complex or, if the builder is deemed to have made a supply of an addition, the addition.

Subsection.Text: A rebate under this section in respect of a residential complex or an addition to it shall not be paid to a builder, unless the builder files an application for the rebate within two years after the end of the month in which tax referred to in subsection (1) is deemed to have been paid by the builder.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 274.1:

Section.Text: If

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time before January 1, 2008, a supplier and a recipient enter into an agreement for a taxable supply of property or a service,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the supplier and the recipient at a later time either directly or indirectly

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: vary or alter the agreement for the supply, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: terminate the agreement and enter into one or more new agreements with each other or with other persons and under one or more of those agreements the supplier supplies, and the recipient receives, one or more supplies that includes all or substantially all the property or service referred to in paragraph (a),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the supplier, the recipient and, where applicable, the other persons are not dealing with each other at arm’s length at the time the agreement is entered into or at the later time,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: tax under subsection 165(1) or section 218 in respect of the supply referred to in paragraph (a) would have been calculated at the rate of 6% or 7%, as the case may be, on all or part of the value of the consideration for the supply attributable to the property or service in the absence of the variation, alteration or termination of the agreement,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: tax under subsection 165(1) or section 218 in respect of the supply made under the varied or altered agreement or made under any of the new agreements would, in the absence of this section, be calculated at the rate of 5% on any part of the value of the consideration for the supply, attributable to any part of the property or service, on which tax, in respect of the supply referred to in paragraph (a), was initially calculated at the rate of 6% or 7%, as the case may be, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the variation or alteration of the agreement or the entering into of the new agreements may not reasonably be considered for both the supplier and the recipient to have been undertaken or arranged primarily for bona fide purposes other than to benefit in any manner from the rate change,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the following rule applies

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: tax under subsection 165(1) or section 218 in respect of the supply made under the varied or altered agreement or made under any of the new agreements shall be calculated at the rate at which tax would have been calculated under paragraph (d) on any part of the value of the consideration, referred to in paragraph (e), attributable to any part of the property or service.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any agreement varied, altered, terminated or entered into on or after October 30, 2007.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments as a Result of the GST/HST Rate Reduction

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 12(1)(a) of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $4.67 for each chargeable emplanement included in the service, to a maximum of $9.33, if

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 12(1)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $4.90 for each chargeable emplanement included in the service, to a maximum of $9.80, if

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 12(1)(d) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: $8.34 for each chargeable emplanement included in the service, to a maximum of $16.68, if

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 12(2)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $8.34 for each chargeable emplanement by an individual on an aircraft used to transport the individual to a destination outside Canada but within the continental zone, to a maximum of $16.68, if

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply in respect of any air transportation service that includes a chargeable emplanement on or after January 1, 2008 and for which any consideration is paid or becomes payable on or after that day.
The portion of the definition taxed tobacco in section 58.1 of the Excise Act, 2001 before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: taxed tobacco of a person means cigarettes, tobacco sticks, loose tobacco and cigars, in respect of which duty has been imposed under section 42 before January 1, 2008 at a rate set out in paragraph 1(b), 2(b) or 3(b) of Schedule 1 or in section 4 of that Schedule, as those provisions read on December 31, 2007, and that, at the beginning of January 1, 2008,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Paragraphs 58.2(a) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 0.295 cent per cigarette;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 0.275 cent per tobacco stick;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 0.195 cent per gram of loose tobacco; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: 0.19 cent per cigar.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Section 58.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Tax under this Part in respect of the inventory of all taxed tobacco of a person that is held at the beginning of January 1, 2008 at a separate retail establishment of the person is not payable if that retail establishment holds inventory of 30,000 or fewer units.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Subsection 58.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person liable to pay tax under this Part shall, on or before February 29, 2008, file a return with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner.
Subsection (1) applies to tax that a person is required to pay under section 58.2 of the Act after December 31, 2007.
Subsection 58.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person shall pay to the Receiver General the total tax payable by the person under this Part on or before February 29, 2008.
Subsection (1) applies to tax that a person is required to pay under section 58.2 of the Act after December 31, 2007.
Subparagraphs 216(2)(a)(i) to (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.17 multiplied by the number of cigarettes to which the offence relates,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.127 multiplied by the number of tobacco sticks to which the offence relates,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.116 multiplied by the number of grams of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks to which the offence relates, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.29 multiplied by the number of cigars to which the offence relates, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 216(3)(a)(i) to (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.255 multiplied by the number of cigarettes to which the offence relates,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.19 multiplied by the number of tobacco sticks to which the offence relates,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.174 multiplied by the number of grams of manufactured tobacco other than cigarettes or tobacco sticks to which the offence relates, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: $0.67 multiplied by the number of cigars to which the offence relates, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force on the later of January 1, 2008 and the day on which this Act is assented to.
Paragraphs 240(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.361448 per cigarette that was removed in contravention of that subsection,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.2105 per tobacco stick that was removed in contravention of that subsection, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $207.704 per kilogram of manufactured tobacco, other than cigarettes and tobacco sticks, that was removed in contravention of that subsection.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on the later of January 1, 2008 and the day on which this Act is assented to.
Paragraph 1(b) of Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.425 for each five cigarettes or fraction of five cigarettes contained in any package, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Paragraph 2(b) of Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.06325 per stick, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Paragraph 3(b) of Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $57.85 per kilogram, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.
Section 4 of Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Cigars: $18.50 per 1,000 cigars.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (b) of Schedule 2 to the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 67%, computed on

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force, or are deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2008.

Section.Text: For the purposes of applying the provisions of the Customs Act that provide for the payment of, or the liability to pay, interest in respect of any amount, the amount shall be determined and interest shall be computed on it as though sections 204 to 208 had come into force on January 1, 2008.
